title: calibrachoa humilis
benefits: Calibrachoa humilis has many benefits, such as promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety due to its calming properties, improving sleep quality by regulating the body's circadian rhythms, and boosting the immune system by containing antioxidants that protect against free radicals. It also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce pain and inflammation in the body, and even lower blood pressure. Furthermore, Calibrachoa humilis has been known to possess antimicrobial properties, making it effective against various types of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause infections. By incorporating this plant into one's life, individuals can experience improved overall well-being, increased energy levels, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases, ultimately leading to a healthier and more fulfilling life.
parts: Calibrachoa humilis has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever and rheumatism, due to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids. The flowers of the plant contain anthocyanins and other pigments that have been used traditionally to treat eye problems and skin conditions. The roots of Calibrachoa humilis contain iridoids, which may have anti-inflammatory properties, and are used in some traditional medicine practices to treat digestive issues. Additionally, the stems and leaves of the plant have been reported to contain alkaloids, which may have antimicrobial and antiviral activities.
side_effects: Calibrachoa humilis can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal problems. Ingesting any part of the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to the presence of toxic compounds like calycanthine and calabishine. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap or leaves can lead to skin rashes, redness, and itching, especially in individuals with sensitive skin. Additionally, using calibrachoa humilis extracts as a medicinal treatment without proper guidance can result in interactions with other medications and exacerbate underlying health conditions.
